name: YNX Governance
title: YNX Governance — Protocol proposals and public verification
metaDescription: Review YNX protocol proposals, machine-readable changes, simulations, votes, timelocks, execution verification, conflicts, and emergency actions.
features: Vote, quorum, threshold, and delegation records
Timelock, execution verification, and rollback history
Council conflicts, terms, removals, and emergency-action archive
risks: Governance decisions may change protocol economics, providers, limits, or software behavior.
Voting participation does not guarantee a preferred outcome.
The current candidate is local-only and has no public execution evidence.

faq.question: When does an approved proposal execute?
faq.answer: Only after quorum and threshold checks, the configured timelock, exact execution-manifest validation, and an authorized execution step.

faq.question: Can the Emergency Council move user assets?
faq.answer: No. Emergency authority is limited to temporary pauses for approved targets and expires automatically.

faq.question: Is this governance service publicly deployed?
faq.answer: No. The current release record describes a locally implemented and tested candidate only.
